sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: ESGL Stock Price and Chart — NASDAQ:ESGL
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of ESGL Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: ESGL) stock, including its current price, historical performance, key financial statistics, and company information. It also includes technical analysis with oscillator and moving average summaries, and answers frequently asked questions about the stock's volatility, market capitalization, and dividend policy.

feed.title: MO: Stifel Lowers Price Target for Altria Group While Maintainin
feed.summary: Stifel has lowered its price target for Altria Group (MO) to $68 from $72, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment reflects ongoing analyst evaluations, with other firms like UBS and B of A Securities also having made recent changes to their ratings and price targets for the tobacco giant. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst target still suggests a slight upside from its current price, although GuruFocus' GF Value indicates a potential downside.

feed.title: Why Apple's Earnings Matter More For Tech ETFs Than Growth Stocks
feed.summary: Apple Inc.'s strong fiscal first-quarter earnings highlight a shift in how tech ETFs are perceived, moving from speculative growth vehicles to portfolios emphasizing profit quality, margin durability, and balance-sheet strength, according to Direxion's Ryan Lee. Despite chip shortages, Apple's ability to prioritize high-end devices and leverage its supplier relationships has driven these strong results. This repositioning means that Apple-heavy ETFs are now anchored by profit quality rather than just unit growth, prompting investors to view them as a blend of durability and optionality.

feed.title: Do Wall Street Analysts Like Howmet Aerospace Stock?
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) has shown strong performance, outperforming the S&P 500 and the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF, largely due to its acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing, LLC. The company reported better-than-expected Q3 results and analysts forecast significant EPS growth for the current fiscal year. With a "Strong Buy" consensus from analysts and an average price target offering a 17.3% premium, the outlook for HWM stock appears very positive.

feed.title: Hologic Q1 comes up short of Street expectations ahead of sale to private equity
feed.summary: Hologic's first-quarter results fell short of Wall Street's expectations, with the company reporting adjusted earnings per share of $1.04, 6 cents below forecasts, and sales of $1.05 billion, just under the projected $1.07 billion. Shares remained mostly flat despite the miss as the company prepares for its acquisition by private equity firms Blackstone and TPG, a deal valued at up to $18.3 billion. Due to the pending transaction, Hologic will not provide future guidance or hold a conference call, with a stockholder meeting scheduled for February 5, 2026, to vote on the merger agreement.

feed.title: Potomac Edison Residential Customers in Maryland to Receive Second Round of Energy Relief Bill Credits
feed.summary: Potomac Edison residential customers in Maryland will automatically receive a second round of energy relief bill credits in February 2026, stemming from the state's Legislative Energy Relief Refund program and the Next Generation Energy Act passed in April 2025. These credits aim to help families manage rising electricity costs and are based on the household's average electricity use over the past year. Eligible customers do not need to sign up, as the credits will be applied automatically to their bills.

feed.title: 247,000 Maryland customers to get up to $60.32 off February power bills
feed.summary: Potomac Edison will provide automatic energy relief bill credits to over 247,000 Maryland residential customers in February 2026, as part of the state's Legislative Energy Relief Refund program. The credit amounts will range from $45.32 to $60.32, tiered by annual household electricity usage, and are funded through Maryland's renewable energy laws without being charged back to customers. This initiative, created by the Next Generation Energy Act of April 2025, aims to help families manage rising energy costs.

feed.title: Barrington Raises Price Target on Snap-on to $380-$385 Range From $365, Keeps Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Barrington has increased its price target for Snap-on (SNA) to $380-$385, up from $365, while maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the company's financial prospects, as analysts continue to evaluate its performance and market position. Another firm, Baird, recently adjusted its price target for Snap-on to $370 from $365, though it maintained a Neutral rating.

feed.title: CL Earnings: Colgate-Palmolive Stock Skyrockets over 5% as Sales Growth Beats Estimates on LATAM Rebound
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive shares rose over 5% after reporting better-than-expected organic sales growth of 2.2% in the fourth quarter, largely due to a successful recovery in Latin America. Despite a significant non-cash impairment charge related to its skin health business, the company's core operations showed resilience. Looking ahead, Colgate-Palmolive provided a cautious 2026 outlook with expected organic sales growth between 1% and 4%, while planning increased advertising spend and leveraging its strong operating cash flow.

feed.title: Duke Energy’s Storm Recovery Highlights Grid Reliability And Valuation Trade Offs
feed.summary: Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) successfully restored power to over 131,000 customers in the Carolinas following Winter Storm Fern, demonstrating its focus on grid reliability and customer support. The company also offers energy efficiency advice and flexible billing options in response to cold temperatures. This event provides insights for investors on how Duke Energy balances infrastructure maintenance, grid modernization, and customer-focused programs, especially considering its current valuation and interest coverage.

feed.title: Previous Merck & Co. Stock Outlook: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?
feed.summary: Merck & Co. (MRK) has underperformed the S&P 500 and the iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF over the past year, despite outperforming both year-to-date in 2026. This underperformance is attributed to regulatory scrutiny, pricing pressures, impending patent cliffs, and slower sales for key drugs. Despite these challenges, analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for MRK, with several suggesting a "Strong Buy" and a potential upside of 20% to 24.6% from current levels.

feed.title: Duke Energy encourages customers to take control of their energy usage and save money as cold spell arrives in Florida this weekend – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Duke Energy is advising its customers in Florida to prepare for a significant cold spell, with temperatures potentially dropping 20-25 degrees below average. The company encourages customers to utilize various programs and simple energy-saving solutions to manage increased energy consumption and save money on their bills during this period. Resources include the EnergyWise® Home Program, Time-of-use Rate Program, and practical tips such as adjusting thermostats and sealing air leaks.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Eastman Chemical Q4 2025 sees steady EPS, revenue miss
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company reported mixed results for Q4 2025, meeting EPS expectations at $0.75 but missing revenue forecasts at $1.97 billion, leading to a slight stock decline. The company is focusing on significant cost reductions, innovation in its product lines, and circular economy initiatives to drive earnings improvement in 2026, targeting an EPS range of $5.50 to $6.00. Challenges include a weak economic environment, overcapacity in the Chinese market, and ongoing macroeconomic pressures, but long-term strategies like the E2P project and circular economy platforms offer potential for recovery and structural improvement.

feed.title: ExxonMobil reports 14% drop in Q4 net profit
feed.summary: ExxonMobil reported a 14% decrease in net profit for Q4 2025, reaching $6.5bn, largely due to lower crude prices, weaker chemical margins, and increased depreciation costs. Despite this, the company achieved its highest annual upstream production in over 40 years and maintained significant shareholder distributions, including $37.2bn for the full year 2025. ExxonMobil's CEO, Darren Woods, stated that the company's transformation is creating a more resilient business with stronger earnings power, grounded in disciplined capital allocation and execution excellence.

feed.title: The Golden Standard: Newmont's "Viljoen Era" Redefines Mining Efficiency as Gold Hits $5,100
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation, under new CEO Natascha Viljoen, has achieved record-breaking 70% profit margins in January 2026, coinciding with gold prices hitting $5,100 per ounce. This success is attributed to "Project Catalyst," an AI-driven cost-optimization program and strategic divesting of non-core assets. The company's stock price has surged 180% in the last year, positioning Newmont as a leader in efficient, high-margin mining, despite potential future challenges like windfall taxes and valuation concerns.

feed.title: Here’s Why Giverny Capital Asset Management Sold Fiserv (FISV) in 2025
feed.summary: Giverny Capital Asset Management sold its stake in Fiserv (FISV) in the fourth quarter of 2025, ahead of a significant share price drop. The firm noted that the former CEO's abrupt departure to a government role and subsequent comments from the new CEO about overly optimistic guidance and underinvestment in technology were key factors. Giverny felt other AI stocks offered more promising returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Entergy January winter storm update – 1/30/2026, 7 a.m.
feed.summary: Entergy provides an update on the January 2026 winter storm, reporting that 68% of affected customers have had power restored, totaling approximately 116,200 customers. Crews are focusing on the hardest-hit areas, primarily Louisiana and Mississippi, where significant damage to infrastructure has occurred. The company anticipates restoring power to all remaining customers who can safely receive it by the end of the weekend, while also detailing workforce mobilization and energy-saving tips for customers.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Stock Surges 17% on Strong Earnings Reaction
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) stock surged 17% after reporting strong Q4 2025 adjusted EPS and an optimistic 2026 forecast exceeding analyst expectations. The company also announced significant business model changes, including assigned seating and bag fees, as part of a transformation effort. This strategic pivot and solid financial guidance have driven investor enthusiasm despite a slight revenue miss for Q4 2025.

feed.title: Verizon Posts Best Subscriber Growth Since 2019
feed.summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE: VZ) reported strong fourth-quarter financial results, beating analyst estimates for revenue and adjusted EPS. The company achieved its best postpaid phone net additions since 2019 and provided an optimistic outlook for fiscal year 2026, including significant free cash flow growth. This performance indicates a positive shift as Verizon implements aggressive strategies to compete with rivals like AT&T and T-Mobile.

feed.title: Here’s Why Giverny Capital Asset Management Sold Align Technology (ALGN)
feed.summary: Giverny Capital Asset Management, LLC sold its stake in Align Technology (ALGN) during Q4 2025, citing concerns about orthodontist resistance to clear aligners impacting market share for teens, along with annoyance at excessive stock-based compensation for management despite a belief in the product's quality. The firm noted that its portfolio underperformed the S&P 500 in Q4 2025 due to an underweight in large tech and overweight in smaller niche leaders, with the market focusing heavily on AI investments. Align Technology's stock closed at $164.12 per share on January 29, 2026, and its presence in hedge fund portfolios declined in Q3 2025.

feed.title: Here’s What Hit Fiserv (FISV) in Q4
feed.summary: Hotchkis & Wiley Large Cap Disciplined Value Fund reported that Fiserv, Inc. (NASDAQ: FISV) was a leading detractor in its Q4 2025 investor letter. Fiserv's shares lost 70.64% of their value over the last 52 weeks, despite the company's attractive valuation due to its scale and potential for double-digit earnings growth. The fund attributes the underperformance to a significant FY25 outlook cut and a disappointing Q3 in the Financial Solutions segment, noting that 2026 is expected to be a transition year for the company under its new CEO.

feed.title: Franklin Resources Inc (NYSE:BEN) Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings Beat on Robust Client Inflows
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc (NYSE:BEN) reported a significant earnings beat for Q1 2026, driven by robust client inflows and strong investment income. The company exceeded analyst expectations with adjusted diluted EPS of $0.70 and operating revenues of $2.33 billion. This strong performance led to record Assets Under Management (AUM) of $1.684 trillion and positive long-term net inflows, particularly in alternative investments and the ETF platform.

feed.title: Franklin Resources: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc. reported strong fiscal first-quarter earnings, with net income of $255.5 million and adjusted earnings of 70 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street's expectations of 55 cents per share. The investment manager also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $2.33 billion against an expected $2.15 billion. The company's stock has seen significant growth, rising over 8% year-to-date and 29% in the last 12 months.

feed.title: Is Copart Stock the Silent Money Machine Everyone’s Sleeping On?
feed.summary: Copart Inc. is presented as a quietly successful stock, positioned as a "silent money machine" in the salvage-auction industry. The article highlights its strong business model, digital-first approach, and consistent growth, despite not being a meme stock or trending like AI or crypto. It clarifies that while it may seem boring on the surface, the underlying trends of increasing vehicle complexity, extreme weather, and global demand for used parts create a powerful tailwind for the company, making it an attractive long-term investment for those who prioritize steady compounding over rapid, speculative gains.

feed.title: RBC Trims Price Target on Sherwin-Williams to $390 From $395, Keeps Outperform Rating
feed.summary: RBC Capital Markets has reduced its price target for Sherwin-Williams (NYSE: SHW) to $390 from $395, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst broader concerns about consumer companies and potential distress in the housing market. The paint and coatings company recently reported Q4 2025 earnings and provided a 2026 profit forecast that fell below analyst estimates, citing weak consumer demand.

feed.title: SolarEdge starts shipping US-made residential inverters to Europe
feed.summary: SolarEdge Technologies has begun exporting US-manufactured residential inverter products from its Austin, Texas facility to European markets, including Italy, France, and the Netherlands. These shipments, which started in late 2025, utilize a "single SKU" design to simplify inventory for installers. The company also anticipates commencing shipments of commercial and industrial PV products from its Florida facility in early 2026.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Boosts Broadcom (AVGO) Price Target on New Growth Catalysts
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Broadcom (AVGO) to $430 from $410 and upgraded the stock to Overweight, citing a recent share decline and new growth catalysts expected through 2026. The bank estimates significant growth in Broadcom's AI semiconductor revenue for 2026 and 2027, along with modest double-digit year-over-year growth for its Infrastructure Software business in FY2026. Broadcom is identified as a promising QQQ stock, although the article suggests other AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: Progressive stock price target lowered to $225 by KBW on auto growth concerns
feed.summary: KBW has lowered its price target for Progressive Corp. (NYSE:PGR) to $225 from $252, maintaining a Market Perform rating due to concerns over "moderating auto PIF growth." This reduction reflects a 10.7% decrease from the prior target. Other analysts have recently presented mixed views, with BofA Securities raising its target to $334 with a Buy rating, while BMO Capital and Morgan Stanley lowered their targets.

feed.title: Lennox International stock price target lowered to $450 by Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reduced its price target for Lennox International (NYSE:LII) to $450 from $475, while maintaining an Underweight rating. This revision follows Lennox's Q4 2025 results, which showed continued volume softness and demand weakness, leading to a derating of the stock. Despite near-term challenges and mixed financial performance, Lennox has demonstrated long-term financial resilience by consistently paying dividends for 27 years with 16 consecutive increases.

feed.title: HP Inc. Stock: Analyst Estimates & Ratings
feed.summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) has significantly underperformed the broader market and the technology sector over the past year and year-to-date, with shares declining 40.5%. The company faces challenges due to a cautious outlook from Morgan Stanley on the U.S. IT hardware sector, citing weakening business demand and rising component costs. Analysts currently have a consensus "Hold" rating on HPQ, with a mean price target of $25.30, suggesting a potential upside.

feed.title: American Express and Mastercard post strong earnings. Political risks loom
feed.summary: American Express and Mastercard have reported strong fourth-quarter and full-year earnings with double-digit revenue growth, despite concerns about rising credit card debt and potential political intervention like a proposed 10% cap on rates. Both companies saw increased consumer spending and healthy net income growth, with American Express raising its dividend and Mastercard announcing workforce reductions. The stock sell-off reflects market anxiety over valuation, political risks, and the broader macroeconomic environment rather than the companies' operational performance.

feed.title: RBC Raises Price Target on L3Harris Technologies to $365 From $360, Keeps Sector Perform Rating
feed.summary: RBC Capital Markets has increased its price target for L3Harris Technologies (LHX) to $365 from $360, while maintaining a "Sector Perform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook on the aerospace and defense company's valuation. The report comes amidst various analyst adjustments for L3Harris, following recent earnings and business updates.

feed.title: I Need a Zevo! Fans Are Empowered to Trap Bugs Like a Hero in Zevo’s New Super Bowl Campaign
feed.summary: Zevo, a pest solutions leader and the Official Bug Defense Partner of Super Bowl LX, has launched its "I Need a Zevo" campaign. The campaign aims to empower hosts to eliminate flying insects with Zevo's trap, featuring NFL quarterback Trevor Lawrence and his wife, Marissa. Zevo seeks to redefine the MVP by making consumers the "hero of their home team," promoting stress-free hosting without pests.

feed.title: TD Cowen Bullish on Constellation Energy (CEG) After Calpine Acquisition
feed.summary: TD Cowen initiated coverage of Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) with a Buy rating and a $440 price target, citing benefits from the Calpine Corporation acquisition and anticipated continuous power contraction. The firm believes strong demand for power in the U.S. and the favorable outcome of the DoJ settlement contribute to a bullish outlook for CEG. However, the article suggests that certain AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk.

feed.title: Benjamin Swinburne to Join Disney as Executive Vice President of Investor Relations and Corporate Strategy
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company announced that Benjamin Swinburne will join as Executive Vice President of Investor Relations and Corporate Strategy, reporting to CFO Hugh F. Johnston. Swinburne, previously Managing Director and Head of US Media Research at Morgan Stanley, will lead Disney's investor relations and oversee long-term strategic planning and market analysis. This move aims to leverage his extensive industry experience to reinforce Disney's strategic vision and shareholder value.

feed.title: Chubb Releases 2026 Flood Risk and Resilience Survey
feed.summary: Chubb has released its 2026 Flood Risk and Resilience Survey, which highlights persistent gaps in the perception, insurance coverage, and mitigation of flood exposure among high-net-worth homeowners, commercial businesses, agents, and brokers. The survey identifies "awareness," "coverage," and "information action" gaps, noting that while flood risk linked to intense rainfall is increasing across broader geographies, many property owners underestimate their personal vulnerability and lack adequate flood insurance or mitigation strategies. The report emphasizes the critical need for separate flood insurance and better implementation of available tools and technologies for comprehensive flood risk management.

feed.title: Franklin Resources Posts Strong Profit Growth and Inflows
feed.summary: Franklin Resources (BEN) reported a significant increase in profitability for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, with net income more than doubling to $255.5 million. This growth was driven by $28.0 billion in long-term net inflows and record assets under management, with alternatives being a major growth engine. An AI analyst rates BEN as "Outperform" due to strong earnings call performance and strategic focus, despite moderate financial performance and valuation.

feed.title: Looking Into CVS Health Corp's Recent Short Interest
feed.summary: CVS Health Corp's (NYSE: CVS) short interest has decreased by 6.01% since its last report, with 21.67 million shares sold short, representing 1.72% of its tradable shares. It would take traders an average of 4.33 days to cover their short positions. Despite the decline, which could signal more bullish sentiment, CVS's short interest is still lower than the peer group average of 9.52%.

feed.title: Analysts Have Conflicting Sentiments on These Industrial Goods Companies: ManpowerGroup (MAN), Honeywell International (HON) and GE Vernova Inc. (GEV)
feed.summary: Analysts are presenting conflicting sentiments on several industrial goods companies, including ManpowerGroup (MAN), Honeywell International (HON), and GE Vernova Inc. (GEV). While ManpowerGroup received a "Hold" rating from William Blair with a $34.33 price target, Honeywell International was given a "Sell" rating by Bank of America Securities with a $205.00 price target. Conversely, GE Vernova Inc. garnered a "Buy" rating from Oppenheimer, boasting an $871.00 price target and a general "Moderate Buy" consensus.

feed.title: Honeywell Q4 Earnings Review: Margin Expansion Is Just Beginning (NASDAQ:HON)
feed.summary: Honeywell International (HON) reported strong Q4 results, with a 'Buy' rating driven by robust organic orders, a record backlog, and accelerated plans for its Aerospace spin-off. The company's FY2026 guidance is optimistic, projecting organic growth between 3-7% and significant Free Cash Flow growth, supporting future deleveraging. Post-separation, Honeywell is expected to see improved margins and valuation re-rating, with potential upside from a Quantinuum IPO, suggesting a 24% upside to $282.67 per share by FY2027.

feed.title: Expedia Group and Affirm deepen partnership, with US exclusivity and plans for further expansion
feed.summary: Expedia Group and Affirm have expanded their multi-year partnership, making Affirm the exclusive Buy Now, Pay Later provider for lodging and packages across Expedia Group's US brands including Expedia, Hotels.com, and Vrbo. The partnership will also extend to Canadian travelers soon, offering flexible payment plans up to 24 months with 0% APR options for eligible US customers. This collaboration aims to provide travelers with more payment flexibility and transparency as travel planning evolves.

feed.title: BofA Awards $500,000 Grant to FIND Regional Food Bank
feed.summary: Bank of America has awarded a $500,000 grant to FIND Regional Food Bank to finalize its capital campaign for a new 40,000 square-foot warehouse on its Food Security Campus. This grant will help fund a facility with extensive cold storage and a sustainable rooftop solar array, crucial for disaster response and resiliency. The new warehouse will nearly triple FIND's storage capacity, allowing it to meet record-level demand, increase fresh produce distribution by 60%, and provide workforce development training.

feed.title: Travelers and National Trust Take Resilience Initiative to Los Angeles
feed.summary: Travelers and the National Trust for Historic Preservation have expanded their "Travelers Across America" initiative to Los Angeles, focusing on wildfire mitigation at the historic Eames House. This project aims to develop a scalable model for regional resilience, addressing the urgent need for communities to prepare for severe events. The initiative, celebrating America's 250th anniversary, also includes efforts in New Orleans, Connecticut, and Minnesota to preserve historic landmarks and build stronger, more resilient communities.

feed.title: Travelers: FY2025 Results Reinforce Long-Term Dividend Appeal (NYSE:TRV)
feed.summary: Travelers (TRV) closed FY2025 with strong underwriting results, demonstrating its ability to manage catastrophe losses while maintaining solid margins and earnings stability. The company's capital allocation strategy, which includes share repurchases and consistent dividend growth, reinforces its long-term dividend appeal. Despite potential challenges in 2026, Travelers' fundamental strength and capital discipline support a BUY rating for long-term investors.

feed.title: PPG partners with Dubai’s Jebel Ali Free Zone on tree-planting initiative
feed.summary: PPG partnered with Dubai’s Jebel Ali Free Zone (JAFZA) for a tree-planting initiative, reinforcing its commitment to environmental stewardship. Over 80 PPG employees planted 500 native trees in an industrial area of JAFZA, aiming to improve air quality and enhance biodiversity. This initiative, the first by a private company in the zone, supports Dubai's sustainability goals.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:LHX) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, beating EPS expectations with $2.86. The company discussed its strategic realignment, including the divestment of a civil space propulsion business and plans for an IPO of its Missile Solutions business in 2026, anticipating double-digit growth. L3Harris also provided optimistic 2026 guidance, expecting 7% organic revenue growth and $3 billion in free cash flow, emphasizing its commitment to operational excellence and meeting customer demands.

feed.title: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) Lowered to "Hold" Rating by Zacks Research
feed.summary: Zacks Research has downgraded Cummins (NYSE:CMI) from a "strong-buy" to a "hold" rating, citing mixed analyst consensus with an average "Moderate Buy" rating but an average price target below the current share price. Despite the downgrade, Cummins exceeded quarterly EPS estimates, though revenue saw a slight year-over-year decrease. Insider selling by key executives was also noted in recent transactions.

feed.title: BrightSpire Capital Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividends
feed.summary: BrightSpire Capital, Inc. announced the tax treatment for its 2025 common stock dividends, detailing four quarterly payments of $0.16 per share, all categorized as a return of capital. The company is a publicly traded commercial real estate (CRE) credit REIT focused on diverse CRE debt investments. Additional dividend information and company details are available on their investor relations website.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Company Foundation Donates $250,000 to Support Winter Storm Recovery in Middle Tennessee
feed.summary: The Tractor Supply Company Foundation has donated $250,000 to the United Way of Greater Nashville's Winter Storm Recovery Fund following a historic winter storm in Middle Tennessee. This donation provides crucial seed money to aid recovery efforts, including addressing immediate needs for food, supplies, cleanup, and debris removal. Tractor Supply is also collaborating with the Nashville Office of Emergency Management to supply emergency items to residents still without power.

feed.title: Global Partners Declares Q4 2025 Cash Distribution Update
feed.summary: Global Partners LP announced a cash distribution of $0.76 per common unit for Q4 2025, payable on February 13, 2026. The company also issued a qualified notice regarding U.S. tax withholding on distributions to non-U.S. investors. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GLP as Neutral due to financial challenges despite some strengths.
